# programs.quickshell

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `programs.quickshell`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `programs.quickshell.activeConfig` | `null or string` | The name of the config to use. If `null`, quickshell will attempt to use a config located in `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/quickshell` instead of one of the named sub-directories. |
| `programs.quickshell.configs` | `attribute set of absolute path` | A set of configs to include in the quickshell config directory. The key is the name of the config. The configuration that quickshell should use can be specified with the `activeConfig` option. |
| `programs.quickshell.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable quickshell, a flexible QtQuick-based desktop shell toolkit.. |
| `programs.quickshell.package` | `null or package` | The quickshell package to use. |
| `programs.quickshell.systemd.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable quickshell systemd service. |
| `programs.quickshell.systemd.target` | `string` | The systemd target that will automatically start quickshell. If you set this to a WM-specific target, make sure that systemd integration for that WM is enabled (e.g. `wayland.windowManager.hyprland.systemd.enable`). **This is typically true by default**. |
